body.title: uurecall: literal search across logs/tickets/code (semantic-only is wrong)
body.result: Rewrote uurecall to search 4 sources: CC.0 logs (literal), tickets (id/title/desc), git grep, semantic recall. De-dups ticket IDs across sources. Cap 5 per source.
body.intention: I intend that uurecall returns literal matches from CC.0 logs, ticket content, and code files — 'everything you need to sort what we talked about' — before falling back to semantic results.
body.description:
uurecall currently calls Librarian recall() which does semantic/embedding search only. This misses the primary use case: finding what we actually said and wrote about a term.

Example failure: `uurecall T-inference-proxy-mini-rack` returns semantically related but lexically unrelated tickets (T-model-eval-harness, T-cheap-model-minions). grep on CC.0/2026-06-07.md finds 8 literal hits immediately.

Intended behavior: 'everything you need to sort what we talked about for any search term — code, past conversations, tickets in the DB.'

Fix: search in order:
1. CC.0 chat logs (`~/.unseen_university/logs/CC.0/*.md`) — literal grep, show line + context
2. Tickets (clan.memories TICKETS_ROOT) — id/title/description match
3. Code files (git grep in repo root) — show file:line + snippet
4. Semantic search via Librarian recall() — existing behavior, label clearly as 'semantic'

De-duplicate ticket hits (same ticket appearing in log + ticket DB). Cap each source at 5 results, show source label.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/uurecall.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat-file reads for logs, Postgres for tickets
**Scope boundary:** IN: rewrite uurecall.py to search all 4 sources. OUT: indexing logs into Librarian (separate ticket), uuresearch changes.
**Completion criteria:** `uurecall T-inference-proxy-mini-rack` returns the ticket description AND at least one CC.0 log hit with the literal string. Zero false-only-semantic results for literal ticket IDs.
